Racosperma, a genus of flowering plants in the family Fabaceae, is a type of shrub or tree native to Australia and New Guinea. The health benefits of Racosperma spp. include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Racosperma spp. include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
